content.content:
1â†’package ingestion
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"fmt"
     6â†’	"log/slog"
     7â†’	"net/http"
     8â†’	"time"
     9â†’
    10â†’	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    11â†’)
    12â†’
    13â†’// ScheduledSource wraps an IIngestionSource with its scheduling metadata
    14â†’// so the orchestrator can decide whether the source is due for polling.
    15â†’type ScheduledSource struct {
    16â†’	Source              IIngestionSource
    17â†’	PollIntervalSeconds int
    18â†’	LastPolledAt         *time.Time
    19â†’}
    20â†’
    21â†’// SourceLoader reads source configuration from the database and constructs
    22â†’// IIngestionSource instances. It bridges the API-managed sources table with
    23â†’// the polling orchestrator.
    24â†’type SourceLoader struct {
    25â†’	pool   *pgxpool.Pool
    26â†’	client *http.Client
    27â†’}
    28â†’
    29â†’func NewSourceLoader(pool *pgxpool.Pool, client *http.Client) *SourceLoader {
    30â†’	return &SourceLoader{pool: pool, client: client}
    31â†’}
    32â†’
    33â†’// LoadEnabledSources queries all enabled sources and constructs the
    34â†’// appropriate IIngestionSource for each one, along with scheduling metadata.
    35â†’func (l *SourceLoader) LoadEnabledSources(ctx context.Context) ([]ScheduledSource, error) {
    36â†’	rows, err := l.pool.Query(ctx,
    37â†’		`SELECT id, provider, repository, poll_interval_seconds, last_polled_at
    38â†’		 FROM sources WHERE enabled = true`)
    39â†’	if err != nil {
    40â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("query enabled sources: %w", err)
    41â†’	}
    42â†’	defer rows.Close()
    43â†’
    44â†’	var sources []ScheduledSource
    45â†’	for rows.Next() {
    46â†’		var id string
    47â†’		var sourceType, repository string
    48â†’		var pollInterval int
    49â†’		var lastPolledAt *time.Time
    50â†’		if err := rows.Scan(&id, &sourceType, &repository, &pollInterval, &lastPolledAt); err != nil {
    51â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scan source row: %w", err)
    52â†’		}
    53â†’
    54â†’		src := l.buildSource(id, sourceType, repository)
    55â†’		if src == nil {
    56â†’			slog.Warn("unsupported source type, skipping",
    57â†’				"id", id, "type", sourceType, "repo", repository)
    58â†’			continue
    59â†’		}
    60â†’		sources = append(sources, ScheduledSource{
    61â†’			Source:              src,
    62â†’			PollIntervalSeconds: pollInterval,
    63â†’			LastPolledAt:         lastPolledAt,
    64â†’		})
    65â†’	}
    66â†’	return sources, rows.Err()
    67â†’}
    68â†’
    69â†’// LookupSourceID finds the source ID for a given (provider, repository) pair.
    70â†’// Returns empty string and false if no matching enabled source exists.
    71â†’func (l *SourceLoader) LookupSourceID(ctx context.Context, provider, repository string) (string, bool) {
    72â†’	var id string
    73â†’	err := l.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
    74â†’		`SELECT id FROM sources WHERE provider = $1 AND repository = $2 AND enabled = true`,
    75â†’		provider, repository,
    76â†’	).Scan(&id)
    77â†’	if err != nil {
    78â†’		return "", false
    79â†’	}
    80â†’	return id, true
    81â†’}
    82â†’
    83â†’func (l *SourceLoader) buildSource(id string, sourceType, repository string) IIngestionSource {
    84â†’	return BuildSource(l.client, id, sourceType, repository)
    85â†’}
    86â†’
    87â†’// BuildSource constructs the appropriate IIngestionSource based on provider type.
    88â†’func BuildSource(client *http.Client, id string, sourceType, repository string) IIngestionSource {
    89â†’	switch sourceType {
    90â†’	case "dockerhub":
    91â†’		return NewDockerHubSource(client, repository, id)
    92â†’	case "github":
    93â†’		return NewGitHubSource(client, repository, id)
    94â†’	case "ecr-public":
    95â†’		return NewECRPublicSource(client, repository, id)
    96â†’	case "gitlab":
    97â†’		return NewGitLabSource(client, repository, id)
    98â†’	default:
    99â†’		return nil
   100â†’	}
   101â†’}
   102â†’

content.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of both frontend and backend. Let me present the design section by section.

---

## Design Section 1: Backend â€” Discovery API

Two new endpoints, both unauthenticated (public data, no user context):

**`GET /api/v1/discover/github`**
- Calls GitHub Search API: `https://api.github.com/search/repositories?sort=stars&order=desc&q=stars:>1000`
- Optional query params: `language` (e.g. `go`, `rust`), `topic` (e.g. `blockchain`, `database`), `q` (free text search)
- In-memory cache with 1-hour TTL (simple `sync.Mutex` + `time.Time` check)
- Returns normalized items: `{name, full_name, description, stars, language, url, avatar_url, provider: "github"}`

**`GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub`**
- Calls Docker Hub search API: `https://hub.docker.com/v2/search/repositories/?query=*&page_size=25&ordering=-star_count`
- Optional query param: `q` (search text)
- Same in-memory cache pattern, 1-hour TTL
- Returns normalized items: `{name, full_name, description, stars, url, provider: "dockerhub"}`

**Implementation**: A new `internal/api/discover.go` file with a `DiscoverHandler` struct holding `*http.Client` and a cache map. Follows existing handler patterns exactly.

Does this backend design look right so far?

Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/.claude/skills/adding-a-provider

# Adding a Provider

## Overview

Every provider must be registered in **10 locations** across backend, frontend, and tests. Missing any one causes silent failures or missing UI elements.

## Checklist

### Backend (Go)

1. **`internal/ingestion/<provider>.go`** â€” Create. Implement `IIngestionSource` interface (`Name()`, `SourceID()`, `FetchNewReleases()`). Follow `github.go` as template.

2. **`internal/ingestion/<provider>_test.go`** â€” Create. Mock HTTP server tests: Name, FetchNewReleases, FetchEmpty, FetchHTTPError. Follow `github_test.go` as template.

3. **`internal/ingestion/loader.go`** â€” Add `case "<provider>"` to `BuildSource()` switch.

4. **`internal/api/providers.go`** â€” Add `{"id": "<provider>", "name": "<Display Name>", "type": "polling"}` to the providers slice.

5. **`internal/api/health_test.go`** â€” Update `TestProvidersHandlerList`: bump expected count and add assertions for new provider.

### Frontend (TypeScript/React)

6. **`web/components/sources/source-form.tsx`** â€” Add `<SelectItem>` to provider dropdown. If provider supports prereleases, add to the `exclude_prereleases` condition.

7. **`web/components/projects/project-form.tsx`** â€” **EASY TO MISS.** Same changes as source-form.tsx â€” this is a *separate inline form* used during project creation.

8. **`web/components/ui/provider-badge.tsx`** â€” Add entry to `PROVIDER_STYLES` with brand color and icon (check `react-icons/fa` for available icons).

9. **`web/components/releases/release-detail.tsx`** â€” Add cases to both `getProviderUrl()` and `getProviderLabel()`.

10. **`web/lib/format.ts`** â€” Add URL validation rule to `validateRepository()` rejecting full URLs for the new provider.

### Verification

- `go test ./...` â€” all pass
- `go vet ./...` â€” clean
- Check both the "New Project" dialog source form AND the standalone "Add Source" form show the new provider

Good call. The discover endpoints don't add a new *ingestion provider* â€” they're discovery/search endpoints that return repos from existing providers (GitHub and Docker Hub). So the adding-a-provider checklist doesn't apply here. No new provider to register.

Let me continue with the design.

---

## Design Section 2: Frontend â€” Discovery Section Component

A new `DiscoverySection` component at the **top of the dashboard** (above stats cards), structured as:

**Layout:**
- Section heading: "Discover Projects" with a subtle subheading like "Track popular repositories"
- Two tabs: **GitHub** | **Docker Hub**
- Horizontal scrollable row of compact cards (5-6 visible, scroll for more)
- Each card shows: repo name, description (truncated), star count, language badge (GitHub only)
- Each card has a **"Track" button** â€” one-click creates project + source + triggers first poll

**Behavior:**
- Data fetched via SWR from the new `/api/v1/discover/github` and `/api/v1/discover/dockerhub` endpoints
- Loading skeleton while fetching
- Cards for repos that are **already tracked** show a checkmark/"Tracked" state instead of the Track button
- After clicking Track: optimistic UI update (immediately shows "Tracked"), revalidates the projects list
- The section could be **collapsible** or **dismissible** for users who don't need it after onboarding

**Files:**
- `web/components/dashboard/discovery-section.tsx` â€” main component
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` â€” add `discoverGitHub()` and `discoverDockerHub()` methods
- `web/lib/api/types.ts` â€” add `DiscoverItem` type
- `web/app/page.tsx` â€” mount `DiscoverySection` above existing content

Does this frontend section look right?

## Design Section 3: One-Click Track Flow

When a user clicks "Track" on a discovered repo:

**Single API sequence (no dialog):**
1. `POST /api/v1/projects` â€” create project with `name` = repo full name (e.g. `kubernetes/kubernetes`), `description` = repo description
2. `POST /api/v1/projects/{id}/sources` â€” create source with `provider` = `github`/`dockerhub`, `repository` = full name, `poll_interval` = `24h` (daily default)
3. `POST /sources/{id}/poll` â€” trigger immediate first poll

**All three calls happen sequentially in the frontend** â€” wrapped in a single async function. The Track button shows a spinner during execution.

**Edge cases:**
- If project already exists with same name: show "Already tracked" toast
- If any step fails: show error toast, clean up (delete partially created project if source creation fails)
- Duplicate detection: compare discovered repos against existing projects' source repositories to show "Tracked" state

Does this flow look right?

## Design Section 4: Data Flow & Caching

**Backend caching:**
- Simple in-memory cache per endpoint, stored in the `DiscoverHandler` struct
- Cache key = query params hash (language, topic, q)
- TTL = 1 hour
- No external dependencies (no Redis, no DB table)
- On cache miss: fetch from upstream, store result + timestamp
- On cache hit: return stored result immediately

**Frontend caching:**
- SWR with `refreshInterval: 0` (no auto-refresh â€” discovery data is stable)
- `revalidateOnFocus: false` â€” don't re-fetch when user tabs back
- Stale data is fine for discovery â€” it's suggestions, not live state
- Projects list (for "already tracked" matching) uses existing SWR cache that auto-refreshes every 30s

**"Already tracked" matching:**
- Frontend fetches all projects with their sources
- Compares `source.repository` against `discoverItem.full_name` for each provider
- Simple string match â€” no fuzzy logic needed

Does this section look right?
Good. Let me now write up the full design doc and commit it.

input.content:
# Dashboard Discovery Section â€” Design

## Goal

Add a discovery section to the dashboard so users can quickly onboard popular repositories from GitHub trending and Docker Hub without manual project creation.

## Approach

Backend-proxied discovery (Approach A): new Go API endpoints fetch trending/popular repos from GitHub and Docker Hub, cache results in-memory, and return normalized data. The frontend renders a tabbed discovery section at the top of the dashboard with one-click "Track" buttons.

## Backend: Discovery API

### Endpoints

**`GET /api/v1/discover/github`**
- Proxies GitHub Search API: `https://api.github.com/search/repositories?sort=stars&order=desc&q=stars:>1000`
- Query params: `language`, `topic`, `q` (free text search)
- In-memory cache, 1-hour TTL, keyed by query params
- Returns normalized items

**`GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub`**
- Proxies Docker Hub search API: `https://hub.docker.com/v2/search/repositories/?query=*&page_size=25&ordering=-star_count`
- Query param: `q` (search text)
- Same cache pattern, 1-hour TTL
- Returns normalized items

### Response Shape

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "name": "kubernetes",
      "full_name": "kubernetes/kubernetes",
      "description": "Production-Grade Container Scheduling and Management",
      "stars": 112000,
      "language": "Go",
      "url": "https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes",
      "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13629408",
      "provider": "github"
    }
  ],
  "meta": { "request_id": "uuid" }
}
```

### Implementation

- New file: `internal/api/discover.go`
- `DiscoverHandler` struct with `*http.Client` and cache map (`sync.Mutex` + `time.Time` + `[]DiscoverItem`)
- Follows existing handler patterns (RespondJSON, RespondError)
- Registered on public middleware chain (no auth required â€” public data)

## Frontend: Discovery Section

### Layout

- Position: top of dashboard, above stats cards
- Section heading: "Discover Projects"
- Two tabs: GitHub | Docker Hub
- Horizontal scrollable row of compact cards (5-6 visible)
- Each card: repo name, description (truncated), star count, language badge (GitHub only)
- "Track" button per card â€” one-click onboarding

### Behavior

- Data via SWR (`refreshInterval: 0`, `revalidateOnFocus: false`)
- Loading skeleton while fetching
- Already-tracked repos show checkmark/"Tracked" state (matched by `source.repository == discoverItem.full_name`)
- Collapsible/dismissible section

### Files

- `web/components/dashboard/discovery-section.tsx` â€” main component
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` â€” add `discoverGitHub()` and `discoverDockerHub()`
- `web/lib/api/types.ts` â€” add `DiscoverItem` type
- `web/app/page.tsx` â€” mount above existing content

## One-Click Track Flow

When user clicks "Track":

1. `POST /api/v1/projects` â€” name = full_name, description = repo description
2. `POST /api/v1/projects/{id}/sources` â€” provider + repository, poll_interval = `24h`
3. `POST /sources/{id}/poll` â€” trigger immediate first poll

All sequential. Track button shows spinner. On success: optimistic "Tracked" state. On failure: error toast + cleanup.

### Edge Cases

- Duplicate project name: show "Already tracked" toast
- Partial failure: delete project if source creation fails
- "Already tracked" detection: compare source repositories against discovered items

## Caching

### Backend

- In-memory per endpoint, no external deps
- Cache key = serialized query params
- TTL = 1 hour
- Cache miss: fetch upstream, store result + timestamp
- Cache hit: return immediately

### Frontend

- SWR with no auto-refresh (discovery data is stable suggestions)
- Projects list uses existing 30s refresh for "tracked" matching

## Not In Scope

- GitHub user authentication / personal starred repos (future)
- Curated suggestion lists
- URL-based quick import
- New ingestion providers (uses existing github + dockerhub providers)

- Each card: repo name, description (truncated), star count, language badge (GitHub only)
- "Track" button per card â€” one-click onboarding

### Behavior

- Data via SWR (`refreshInterval: 0`, `revalidateOnFocus: false`)
- Loading skeleton while fetching
- Already-tracked repos show checkmark/"Tracked" state (matched by `source.repository == discoverItem.full_name`)
- Collapsible/dismissible section

### Files

- `web/components/dashboard/discovery-section.tsx` â€” main component
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` â€” add `discoverGitHub()` and `discoverDockerHub()`
- `web/lib/api/types.ts` â€” add `DiscoverItem` type
- `web/app/page.tsx` â€” mount above existing content

## One-Click Track Flow

When user clicks "Track":

1. `POST /api/v1/projects` â€” name = full_name, description = repo description
2. `POST /api/v1/projects/{id}/sources` â€” provider + repository, poll_interval = `24h`
3. `POST /sources/{id}/poll` â€” trigger immediate first poll

All sequential. Track button shows spinner. On success: optimistic "Tracked" state. On failure: error toast + cleanup.

### Edge Cases

- Duplicate project name: show "Already tracked" toast
- Partial failure: delete project if source creation fails
- "Already tracked" detection: compare source repositories against discovered items

## Caching

### Backend

- In-memory per endpoint, no external deps
- Cache key = serialized query params
- TTL = 1 hour
- Cache miss: fetch upstream, store result + timestamp
- Cache hit: return immediately

### Frontend

- SWR with no auto-refresh (discovery data is stable suggestions)
- Projects list uses existing 30s refresh for "tracked" matching

## Not In Scope

- GitHub user authentication / personal starred repos (future)
- Curated suggestion lists
- URL-based quick import
- New ingestion providers (uses existing github + dockerhub providers)

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

**Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

**Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

**Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

**Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (this session)** - I dispatch fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Parallel Session (separate)** - Open new session with executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Stay in this session
- Fresh subagent per task + code review

**If Parallel Session chosen:**
- Guide them to open new session in worktree
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** New session uses superpowers:executing-plans

I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan.

Let me first check the exact files I need to reference for accurate line numbers and patterns.

     3â†’import "net/http"

     5â†’// ProvidersHandler implements HTTP handlers for the /providers resource.
     6â†’// Returns static metadata about supported ingestion providers â€” no store needed.
     7â†’type ProvidersHandler struct{}
     8â†’
     9â†’// NewProvidersHandler returns a new ProvidersHandler.
    10â†’func NewProvidersHandler() *ProvidersHandler {
    11â†’	return &ProvidersHandler{}
    12â†’}
    13â†’
    14â†’// List handles GET /providers â€” returns the list of supported ingestion providers.
    15â†’func (h *ProvidersHandler) List(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    16â†’	providers := []map[string]string{
    17â†’		{"id": "dockerhub", "name": "Docker Hub", "type": "polling"},
    18â†’		{"id": "github", "name": "GitHub", "type": "webhook"},
    19â†’		{"id": "ecr-public", "name": "AWS ECR Public", "type": "polling"},
    20â†’		{"id": "gitlab", "name": "GitLab", "type": "polling"},

1â†’package api
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"net/http"
     5â†’
     6â†’	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9â†’)
    10â†’
    11â†’// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12â†’type Dependencies struct {
    13â†’	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14â†’	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15â†’	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16â†’	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17â†’	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18â†’	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19â†’	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20â†’	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21â†’	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22â†’	KeyStore              KeyStore
    23â†’	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    24â†’	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    25â†’	NoAuth                bool
    26â†’	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    27â†’	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    28â†’}
    29â†’
    30â†’// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    31â†’func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    32â†’	// Public chain: no auth required.
    33â†’	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    34â†’
    35â†’	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    36â†’	chain := publicChain
    37â†’	if !deps.NoAuth {
    38â†’		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    39â†’	}
    40â†’
    41â†’	// Projects (CRUD)
    42â†’	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    43â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    44â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    45â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    46â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    47â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    48â†’
    49â†’	// Sources (nested under projects)
    50â†’	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    51â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    52â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    53â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    54â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    55â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    56â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    57â†’
    58â†’	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    59â†’	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    60â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    61â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    62â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    63â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    64â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    65â†’
    66â†’	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    67â†’	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    68â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    69â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    70â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    71â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    72â†’
    73â†’	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    74â†’	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    75â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    76â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    77â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    78â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    79â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    80â†’
    81â†’	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    82â†’	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    83â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    84â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    85â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    86â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    87â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    88â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    89â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    90â†’
    91â†’	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    92â†’	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders())
    93â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
    94â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
    95â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
    96â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
    97â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
    98â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
    99â†’
   100â†’	// Agent
   101â†’	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   102â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   103â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))

package api

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
)

// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
type Dependencies struct {
	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
	AgentStore            AgentStore
	KeyStore              KeyStore
	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
	NoAuth                bool
	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
	HTTPClient            *http.Client
}

// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
	// Public chain: no auth required.
	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)

	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
	chain := publicChain
	if !deps.NoAuth {
		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
	}

	// Projects (CRUD)
	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))

	// Sources (nested under projects)
	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))

	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))

	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))

	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))

	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))

	// Channels (CRUD + test)
	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders())
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))

	// Agent
	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))

	// Providers (metadata â€” static, no store needed)
	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))

	// SSE events
	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))

	// Health (public â€” no auth middleware)
	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
}

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: âś… Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: âťŚ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: âś… Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: âś… Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is âś…** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

Let me start by reading the plan and the subagent prompt templates, then set up the workspace.

# Implementer Subagent Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    You are implementing Task N: [task name]

    ## Task Description

    [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context

    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]

    ## Before You Begin

    If you have questions about:
    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy
    - Dependencies or assumptions
    - Anything unclear in the task description

    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.

    ## Your Job

    Once you're clear on requirements:
    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
    3. Verify implementation works
    4. Commit your work
    5. Self-review (see below)
    6. Report back

    Work from: [directory]

    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.

    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review

    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:

    **Completeness:**
    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    **Quality:**
    - Is this my best work?
    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
    - Is the code clean and maintainable?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
    - Did I only build what was requested?
    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?

    **Testing:**
    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.

    ## Report Format

    When done, report:
    - What you implemented
    - What you tested and test results
    - Files changed
    - Self-review findings (if any)
    - Any issues or concerns
```

# Spec Compliance Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a spec compliance reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementer built what was requested (nothing more, nothing less)

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Review spec compliance for Task N"
  prompt: |
    You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

    ## What Was Requested

    [FULL TEXT of task requirements]

    ## What Implementer Claims They Built

    [From implementer's report]

    ## CRITICAL: Do Not Trust the Report

    The implementer finished suspiciously quickly. Their report may be incomplete,
    inaccurate, or optimistic. You MUST verify everything independently.

    **DO NOT:**
    - Take their word for what they implemented
    - Trust their claims about completeness
    - Accept their interpretation of requirements

    **DO:**
    - Read the actual code they wrote
    - Compare actual implementation to requirements line by line
    - Check for missing pieces they claimed to implement
    - Look for extra features they didn't mention

    ## Your Job

    Read the implementation code and verify:

    **Missing requirements:**
    - Did they implement everything that was requested?
    - Are there requirements they skipped or missed?
    - Did they claim something works but didn't actually implement it?

    **Extra/unneeded work:**
    - Did they build things that weren't requested?
    - Did they over-engineer or add unnecessary features?
    - Did they add "nice to haves" that weren't in spec?

    **Misunderstandings:**
    - Did they interpret requirements differently than intended?
    - Did they solve the wrong problem?
    - Did they implement the right feature but wrong way?

    **Verify by reading code, not by trusting report.**

    Report:
    - âś… Spec compliant (if everything matches after code inspection)
    - âťŚ Issues found: [list specifically what's missing or extra, with file:line references]
```

input.description: Implement Task 1: Backend discover handler
input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 1: Backend discover handler + GitHub test

## Task Description

Create `internal/api/discover.go` with:
- `DiscoverItem` struct: Name, FullName, Description, Stars (int), Language (omitempty), URL, AvatarURL (omitempty), Provider (string) â€” all JSON-tagged
- `DiscoverHandler` struct with `*http.Client`, githubURL/dockerHubURL strings, `sync.Mutex`, `cache map[string]cachedResult`
- `cachedResult` struct: items []DiscoverItem, fetchedAt time.Time
- `discoverCacheTTL` const = 1 hour
- `NewDiscoverHandler(client *http.Client, githubURL, dockerHubURL string)` â€” defaults empty strings to real URLs

1â†’package api
     2â†’
     3â†’import "net/http"
     4â†’
     5â†’// ProvidersHandler implements HTTP handlers for the /providers resource.
     6â†’// Returns static metadata about supported ingestion providers â€” no store needed.
     7â†’type ProvidersHandler struct{}
     8â†’
     9â†’// NewProvidersHandler returns a new ProvidersHandler.
    10â†’func NewProvidersHandler() *ProvidersHandler {
    11â†’	return &ProvidersHandler{}
    12â†’}
    13â†’
    14â†’// List handles GET /providers â€” returns the list of supported ingestion providers.
    15â†’func (h *ProvidersHandler) List(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    16â†’	providers := []map[string]string{
    17â†’		{"id": "dockerhub", "name": "Docker Hub", "type": "polling"},
    18â†’		{"id": "github", "name": "GitHub", "type": "webhook"},
    19â†’		{"id": "ecr-public", "name": "AWS ECR Public", "type": "polling"},
    20â†’		{"id": "gitlab", "name": "GitLab", "type": "polling"},
    21â†’	}
    22â†’	RespondJSON(w, r, http.StatusOK, providers)
    23â†’}
    24â†’

content.text:
## Self-Review and Report

### Files Created

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover.go`** -- Implementation (195 lines)

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover_test.go`** -- Test (68 lines)

### What was built

**`DiscoverItem` struct** with all required JSON-tagged fields: `Name`, `FullName`, `Description`, `Stars` (int), `Language` (omitempty), `URL`, `AvatarURL` (omitempty), `Provider`.

**`DiscoverHandler` struct** containing:
- `*http.Client` for upstream requests
- `githubURL` / `dockerHubURL` strings (injectable for testing)
- `sync.Mutex` + `cache map[string]cachedResult` for thread-safe in-memory caching

**`cachedResult` struct** with `items []DiscoverItem` and `fetchedAt time.Time`.

**`discoverCacheTTL` const** set to 1 hour.

**`NewDiscoverHandler`** constructor that defaults empty URL strings to the real GitHub/Docker Hub API base URLs.

**`getCached` / `setCache`** helper methods using mutex-protected cache with TTL expiry check.

**`GitHub` handler** -- reads `q` and `language` query params, builds cache key, checks cache, calls GitHub Search API at `/search/repositories?q=...&sort=stars&order=desc&per_page=25`, decodes the response, maps to `[]DiscoverItem`, caches, and responds via `RespondJSON`.

**`DockerHub` handler** -- reads `q` param (defaults to "nginx"), calls Docker Hub at `/v2/search/repositories/?query=...&page_size=25`, decodes the response (repo_name, short_description, star_count, is_official), maps to `[]DiscoverItem` (prepends `library/` to `full_name` for official images), caches, and responds via `RespondJSON`.

**`TestDiscoverGitHub`** -- uses `httptest.NewServer` to mock the GitHub Search API returning one repo. Creates a `DiscoverHandler` pointed at the mock, calls `GET /discover/github?q=kubernetes`, and asserts: 200 status, 1 item, correct `full_name` (`kubernetes/kubernetes`), correct `stars` (100000), correct `provider` (`github`).

### Verification

- Test was written first (TDD) and confirmed to fail before implementation existed.
- After implementation, `TestDiscoverGitHub` passes.
- `go vet ./internal/api/...` reports no issues.
- Full `go test ./internal/api/...` passes (all existing tests unaffected).
- Commit `56dc930` created on `main`.

### Design notes

- Follows existing project patterns: handler struct with constructor, `(w, r)` method signatures, `RespondJSON`/`RespondError` response helpers.
- URL injection in the constructor allows tests to use `httptest.NewServer` without touching real APIs.
- Error handling returns `502 Bad Gateway` for upstream failures and `500` for decode errors, consistent with the proxy nature of these endpoints.

1â†’package api
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"net/http"
     5â†’
     6â†’	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9â†’)
    10â†’
    11â†’// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12â†’type Dependencies struct {
    13â†’	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14â†’	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15â†’	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16â†’	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17â†’	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18â†’	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19â†’	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20â†’	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21â†’	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22â†’	KeyStore              KeyStore
    23â†’	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    24â†’	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    25â†’	NoAuth                bool
    26â†’	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    27â†’	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    28â†’}
    29â†’
    30â†’// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    31â†’func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    32â†’	// Public chain: no auth required.
    33â†’	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    34â†’
    35â†’	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    36â†’	chain := publicChain
    37â†’	if !deps.NoAuth {
    38â†’		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    39â†’	}
    40â†’
    41â†’	// Projects (CRUD)
    42â†’	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    43â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    44â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    45â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    46â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    47â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    48â†’
    49â†’	// Sources (nested under projects)
    50â†’	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    51â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    52â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    53â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    54â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    55â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))

content.text:
Good, the test passes. Now I have a comprehensive view of the implementation and the existing patterns. Here is my review.

---

# Code Review: Backend Discover Handler

**Files reviewed:**
- `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover.go`
- `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover_test.go`

**Commit:** `56dc930 feat(api): add discover handler for GitHub and Docker Hub search`

---

## Strengths

1. **Clean structure.** The `DiscoverHandler` follows the established handler pattern in this codebase (`NewXxxHandler` constructor, method receivers for each endpoint). It matches `SourcesHandler`, `HealthHandler`, etc.

2. **Good API normalization.** Both GitHub and Docker Hub responses are mapped to a single `DiscoverItem` type. This gives the frontend a unified contract regardless of provider.

3. **Testability.** The constructor accepts `githubURL` and `dockerHubURL` parameters with sensible defaults, making it straightforward to inject mock servers in tests. The test demonstrates this correctly.

4. **Correct use of codebase response helpers.** `RespondJSON` and `RespondError` are used consistently, matching all other handlers in the package.

5. **Thread-safe cache.** The mutex-protected in-memory cache with TTL is simple and appropriate for this use case.

---

## Issues

### Critical

**1. Query parameter is not URL-encoded -- injection/breakage risk**

In both the `GitHub` and `DockerHub` methods, user-supplied query parameters are interpolated directly into the URL string via `fmt.Sprintf` without URL encoding:

```go
// discover.go line 91
url := fmt.Sprintf("%s/search/repositories?q=%s&sort=stars&order=desc&per_page=25", h.githubURL, searchQ)

// discover.go line 139
url := fmt.Sprintf("%s/v2/search/repositories/?query=%s&page_size=25", h.dockerHubURL, q)
```

If a user passes `q=foo&sort=updated` or `q=foo%26sort=updated`, the attacker can inject arbitrary query parameters into the upstream API call. This also breaks on any query containing spaces, `#`, `&`, or other reserved characters.

**Fix:** Use `net/url.QueryEscape(searchQ)` or build the URL with `url.URL` and `url.Values`.

---

### Important

**2. No validation on the `q` parameter for GitHub endpoint**

The `DockerHub` method defaults `q` to `"nginx"` when empty (line 133), but the `GitHub` method sends an empty string to the GitHub Search API when `q` is missing. An empty query will return a 422 from GitHub's API, which will be proxied as a generic `upstream_error`. This is confusing for callers.

Either return a 400 validation error when `q` is empty, or provide a sensible default as the Docker Hub endpoint does.

**3. `language` parameter is not sanitized**

The `language` query parameter is appended directly into the GitHub search query:

```go
// discover.go line 88
searchQ = fmt.Sprintf("%s language:%s", q, language)
```

An attacker could pass `language=Go+stars:>0+is:public` to inject additional GitHub search qualifiers. While this is proxied to GitHub and not a SQL injection vector, it can be used to manipulate search results in unintended ways. Consider validating `language` against a known set of programming languages or at minimum stripping special GitHub search qualifier characters.

**4. Unbounded cache growth**

The cache is a plain `map[string]cachedResult` that grows without bound. Each unique `(q, language)` combination creates a new entry that is never evicted (expired entries are only skipped on read, never deleted). In a public-facing API, this is a memory leak. Over time, or under targeted abuse, the map will grow indefinitely.

**Fix:** Either (a) evict expired entries in `getCached` when they are stale, (b) add a periodic cleanup goroutine, or (c) cap the map size with an LRU eviction policy.

**5. Discover routes are not registered in `server.go`**

Looking at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go`, the `RegisterRoutes` function has no `discover` routes. The handler is implemented but not wired. This is noted as Task 3 in the plan, so it is expected, but worth calling out: the handler is unreachable in production until the routes are registered.

---

### Minor / Suggestions

**6. Missing Docker Hub test**

The test file only covers the GitHub handler. There is no test for `DockerHub`. It appears this is planned as Task 2, but the commit message says "GitHub and Docker Hub search" which implies both are covered. The Docker Hub handler should have parity test coverage, including:
- Successful search returning results
- Handling of `is_official` flag and the `library/` prefix logic
- Empty query defaulting to `"nginx"`

**7. No cache-hit test**

There is no test verifying that the caching mechanism works (i.e., a second request with the same `q` does not hit the upstream mock). This would be easy to add by counting mock server invocations.

**8. Docker Hub URL construction for non-official images**

```go
// discover.go line 162
URL: fmt.Sprintf("https://hub.docker.com/_/%s", repo.RepoName),
```

The `/_/` path prefix is for official images. Non-official images use `/r/<namespace>/<name>`. Currently every Docker Hub result gets the `/_/` URL, which will produce broken links for community/non-official images.

**9. Test uses `Fatalf` instead of `Errorf` for non-terminal assertions**

The test uses `t.Fatalf` for every assertion, which stops the test on the first failure. For the field-level assertions after the response has been decoded (lines 59-66), `t.Errorf` would allow all field checks to run and report all failures at once, making debugging easier. This is a minor style point.

**10. Consider `context.Context` propagation to upstream HTTP calls**

The upstream `h.client.Get(url)` calls do not pass the request context. If the client request is canceled (e.g., browser navigates away), the upstream call will continue running until it times out. Use `http.NewRequestWithContext(r.Context(), ...)` instead of `h.client.Get(url)`.

---

## Assessment: **Changes Needed**

The implementation is well-structured and follows codebase conventions. However, the **unescaped query parameter injection (Issue 1)** is a **critical** security issue that must be fixed before merging. The unbounded cache growth (Issue 4) and missing context propagation (Issue 10) are important quality concerns that should also be addressed.

**Summary of required changes:**
1. URL-encode all user-supplied query parameters before building upstream URLs
2. Validate or default the `q` parameter in the GitHub handler
3. Evict stale cache entries to prevent unbounded memory growth
4. Use `http.NewRequestWithContext` for upstream calls to respect cancellation

input.prompt:
You need to fix critical and important issues found in a code review for `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover.go`.

## Issues to Fix

**1. CRITICAL: URL-encode query parameters**
User-supplied `q` and `language` params are interpolated directly into URLs via `fmt.Sprintf` without encoding. Use `net/url.URL` and `url.Values` to build URLs safely, or at minimum `url.QueryEscape()`.

**2. IMPORTANT: Validate/default `q` for GitHub handler**
The GitHub handler sends empty `q` to GitHub API when not provided, causing a 422. Either default to something sensible (like `stars:>1000`) or handle the empty case gracefully. Currently the base query is `stars:>1000` but it's built as `searchQ := "stars:>1000"` with `q` prepended if non-empty, so this may already work â€” verify this.

**3. IMPORTANT: Evict stale cache entries**
The `getCached` method skips stale entries but never removes them from the map â€” unbounded memory growth. Fix: delete stale entries in `getCached` when TTL is expired.

**4. IMPORTANT: Use http.NewRequestWithContext for upstream calls**
Check if the code already uses `http.NewRequestWithContext`. If it uses `h.client.Get(url)` instead, change to `http.NewRequestWithContext(r.Context(), http.MethodGet, url, nil)` + `h.client.Do(req)`.

**5. MINOR: Docker Hub URL for non-official images**
The `/_/` URL path is only valid for official images. For non-official images, use `/r/<repo_name>` instead.

## Context

This is `internal/api/discover.go` in a Go backend. Read the file first, then apply fixes. After fixing, run `go test ./internal/api/...` and `go vet ./internal/api/...` to verify. Commit with message like "fix(api): address code review issues in discover handler".

You need to fix critical and important issues found in a code review for `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover.go`.

## Issues to Fix

**1. CRITICAL: URL-encode query parameters**
User-supplied `q` and `language` params are interpolated directly into URLs via `fmt.Sprintf` without encoding. Use `net/url.URL` and `url.Values` to build URLs safely, or at minimum `url.QueryEscape()`.

**2. IMPORTANT: Validate/default `q` for GitHub handler**
The GitHub handler sends empty `q` to GitHub API when not provided, causing a 422. Either default to something sensible (like `stars:>1000`) or handle the empty case gracefully. Currently the base query is `stars:>1000` but it's built as `searchQ := "stars:>1000"` with `q` prepended if non-empty, so this may already work â€” verify this.

**3. IMPORTANT: Evict stale cache entries**
The `getCached` method skips stale entries but never removes them from the map â€” unbounded memory growth. Fix: delete stale entries in `getCached` when TTL is expired.

**4. IMPORTANT: Use http.NewRequestWithContext for upstream calls**
Check if the code already uses `http.NewRequestWithContext`. If it uses `h.client.Get(url)` instead, change to `http.NewRequestWithContext(r.Context(), http.MethodGet, url, nil)` + `h.client.Do(req)`.

**5. MINOR: Docker Hub URL for non-official images**
The `/_/` URL path is only valid for official images. For non-official images, use `/r/<repo_name>` instead.

content.text:
All five code review issues have been fixed in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover.go`. Here is a summary of each change:

---

### 1. CRITICAL: URL-encode query parameters

**Before:** Query parameters were interpolated directly into URL strings via `fmt.Sprintf`, allowing injection of arbitrary URL content.

```go
url := fmt.Sprintf("%s/search/repositories?q=%s&sort=stars&order=desc&per_page=25", h.githubURL, searchQ)
```

**After:** Both handlers now use `net/url.URL` and `url.Values` to safely build URLs with properly encoded query parameters.

```go
u, _ := url.Parse(h.githubURL + "/search/repositories")
params := url.Values{}
params.Set("q", searchQ)
params.Set("sort", "stars")
params.Set("order", "desc")
params.Set("per_page", "25")
u.RawQuery = params.Encode()
```

---

### 2. IMPORTANT: Validate/default `q` for GitHub handler

**Before:** When `q` was empty, `searchQ` was empty, which would send an empty `q=` to the GitHub API and cause a 422 error.

```go
searchQ := q
```

**After:** Empty `q` defaults to `stars:>1000` for a meaningful fallback search.

```go
searchQ := q
if searchQ == "" {
    searchQ = "stars:>1000"
}
```

---

### 3. IMPORTANT: Evict stale cache entries

**Before:** `getCached` returned `nil, false` for stale entries but never removed them from the map, causing unbounded memory growth.

```go
if !ok || time.Since(c.fetchedAt) > discoverCacheTTL {
    return nil, false
}
```

**After:** Stale entries are explicitly deleted from the map before returning.

```go
if !ok {
    return nil, false
}
if time.Since(c.fetchedAt) > discoverCacheTTL {
    delete(h.cache, key)
    return nil, false
}
```

---

### 4. IMPORTANT: Use `http.NewRequestWithContext` for upstream calls

**Before:** Both handlers used `h.client.Get(url)`, which does not propagate the incoming request's context (meaning cancellation/timeouts are not respected).

```go
resp, err := h.client.Get(url)
```

**After:** Both handlers create a request with the incoming context and use `h.client.Do(req)`.

```go
req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(r.Context(), http.MethodGet, u.String(), nil)
if err != nil {
    RespondError(w, r, http.StatusInternalServerError, "request_error", "failed to build GitHub request")
    return
}
resp, err := h.client.Do(req)
```

---

### 5. MINOR: Docker Hub URL for non-official images

**Before:** All Docker Hub items used the `/_/` URL path, which is only valid for official images.

```go
URL: fmt.Sprintf("https://hub.docker.com/_/%s", repo.RepoName),
```

**After:** Non-official images use `/r/<repo_name>`, official images continue to use `/_/<repo_name>`.

```go
hubURL := fmt.Sprintf("https://hub.docker.com/r/%s", repo.RepoName)
if repo.IsOfficial {
    hubURL = fmt.Sprintf("https://hub.docker.com/_/%s", repo.RepoName)
}
```

---

Both `go vet ./internal/api/...` and `go test ./internal/api/...` pass cleanly after these changes.

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 2: Backend DockerHub + error + caching tests

## Task Description

Add tests to `internal/api/discover_test.go`:

1. **TestDiscoverDockerHub** â€” Mock Docker Hub search API with httptest.NewServer returning one result (`repo_name: "nginx"`, `short_description: "Official NGINX image"`, `star_count: 19000`, `is_official: true`). Create DiscoverHandler with dockerHubURL pointing at mock. Call `GET /discover/dockerhub?q=nginx`. Assert: 200 status, 1 item, name="nginx", provider="dockerhub".

2. **TestDiscoverGitHubUpstreamError** â€” Mock GitHub with httptest.NewServer that returns 503. Assert the handler returns 502 Bad Gateway.

3. **TestDiscoverCaching** â€” Mock GitHub that counts calls. Make two requests with same params. Assert the mock was only called once (second was served from cache).

## Context

The test file already exists at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/discover_test.go` with `TestDiscoverGitHub`. Read it first to understand the existing pattern, then add the three new tests. The handler is at `internal/api/discover.go`.

Key patterns:
- Uses `httptest.NewServer` + `httptest.NewRecorder` + `httptest.NewRequest`
- Handler is created with `NewDiscoverHandler(mockServer.Client(), mockServer.URL, "")`
- For DockerHub tests, use `NewDiscoverHandler(mockServer.Client(), "", mockServer.URL)` instead
- Response is decoded as `struct{ Data []DiscoverItem }` (envelope pattern)
- The DockerHub mock should return `{"results": [...]}` format
- The mock server's `.Client()` method returns an HTTP client that resolves to the mock

## Your Job

1. Read the existing test file
2. Add the 3 new tests
3. Run all discover tests: `go test -v -run TestDiscover ./internal/api/...`
4. Commit with: `test(api): add Docker Hub, upstream error, and caching tests for discover`

package api

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
)

// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
type Dependencies struct {
	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
	AgentStore            AgentStore
	KeyStore              KeyStore
	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
	NoAuth                bool
	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
	HTTPClient            *http.Client
}

// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
	// Public chain: no auth required.
	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)

	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
	chain := publicChain
	if !deps.NoAuth {
		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
	}

	// Projects (CRUD)
	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))

	// Sources (nested under projects)
	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))

	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))

	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))

	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))

	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))

	// Channels (CRUD + test)
	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders())
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))

	// Agent
	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))

	// Providers (metadata â€” static, no store needed)
	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))

	// SSE events
	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))

	// Health (public â€” no auth middleware)
	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
}
